topic: A person's online friend turns out to live in the same city
raw_output:
[story 1]

The notification popped up, a familiar chime from my feed. It was Maya. I almost flinched, my thumb hovering over the screen, a sudden tightness gripping my chest. Her profile picture, usually a bright, carefree smile, was now a candid shot of a building I recognized immediately—the old brick facade of the library downtown. A slow, cold dread started to pool in my stomach. I clicked the profile, my breath catching in my throat as I scrolled through her recent posts. Then I saw it: a geotag. A pinpoint location right in the heart of my own city. My hands started to shake slightly as I reread the caption, a frantic need to process the impossible washing over me. I quickly minimized the window, the heat rising in my cheeks, wishing the floor would swallow me whole.

[story 2]

I kept my phone face down, pretending to be engrossed in a mundane work email, but every few seconds my eyes darted to the screen. Maya’s latest story was a picture of a coffee shop I frequented, the one with the mismatched chairs by the window. A wave of intense awkwardness washed over me, making my throat feel suddenly dry. I felt a distinct urge to check my own location, a desperate need to confirm if I was dreaming. When I finally did, the coordinates matched mine perfectly. My jaw tightened. I stood up abruptly, pacing the small apartment, my movements jerky and agitated. What did this mean? The realization settled in my gut like a heavy stone, leaving me feeling exposed and painfully scrutinized.

[story 3]

The message arrived with the casual ease of a text, but the content sent a jolt through me. "Guess where I am?" Maya’s simple query was laced with a knowing smirk that made my palms sweat. I stared at the screen, my gaze flickering between the text and my reflection in the darkened monitor. My shoulders immediately hunched up toward my ears, a defensive posture I couldn't seem to shake. I felt a flush creep up my neck, hot and undeniable. I quickly typed a reply, focusing intently on the keyboard, trying to sound breezy, but the words felt clumsy and inadequate. Every nerve ending seemed hyper-aware, cataloging every minute detail of my immediate surroundings, as if waiting for some judgment to manifest.
